root_text.an1.316:1.1: “Ekapuggalo, bhikkhave, loke uppajjamāno uppajjati bahujanaahitāya bahujanaasukhāya, bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an1.316:1.4: So bahujanaṁ saddhammā vuṭṭhāpetvā asaddhamme patiṭṭhāpeti.
root_text.an1.316:1.5: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, ekapuggalo loke uppajjamāno uppajjati bahujanaahitāya bahujanaasukhāya, bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānan”ti.
root_text.an1.317:1.1: “Ekapuggalo, bhikkhave, loke uppajjamāno uppajjati bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya, bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an1.317:1.4: So bahujanaṁ asaddhammā vuṭṭhāpetvā saddhamme patiṭṭhāpeti.
root_text.an1.317:1.5: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, ekapuggalo loke uppajjamāno uppajjati bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya, bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānan”ti.
root_text.an1.318:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ mahāsāvajjaṁ yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, micchādiṭṭhi.
root_text.an1.319:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekapuggalampi samanupassāmi yo evaṁ bahujanaahitāya paṭipanno bahujanaasukhāya, bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, makkhali moghapuriso.
root_text.an1.319:1.2: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, nadīmukhe khippaṁ uḍḍeyya bahūnaṁ macchānaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya anayāya byasanāya;
root_text.an1.319:1.3: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, makkhali moghapuriso manussakhippaṁ maññe loke uppanno bahūnaṁ sattānaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya anayāya byasanāyā”ti.
root_text.an1.320:1.1: “Durakkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye yo ca samādapeti yañca samādapeti yo ca samādapito tathattāya paṭipajjati sabbe te bahuṁ apuññaṁ pasavanti.
root_text.an1.321:1.1: “Svākkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye yo ca samādapeti yañca samādapeti yo ca samādapito tathattāya paṭipajjati sabbe te bahuṁ puññaṁ pasavanti.
root_text.an1.322:1.1: “Durakkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye dāyakena mattā jānitabbā, no paṭiggāhakena.
root_text.an1.323:1.1: “Svākkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye paṭiggāhakena mattā jānitabbā, no dāyakena.
root_text.an1.324:1.1: “Durakkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye yo āraddhavīriyo so dukkhaṁ viharati.
root_text.an1.325:1.1: “Svākkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye yo kusīto so dukkhaṁ viharati.
root_text.an1.326:1.1: “Durakkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye yo kusīto so sukhaṁ viharati.
root_text.an1.327:1.1: “Svākkhāte, bhikkhave, dhammavinaye yo āraddhavīriyo so sukhaṁ viharati.
root_text.an1.328:1.2: evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, appamattakampi bhavaṁ na vaṇṇemi, antamaso accharāsaṅghātamattampi”.
root_text.an1.329:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, appamattakampi muttaṁ duggandhaṁ hoti … appamattakopi kheḷo duggandho hoti … appamattakopi pubbo duggandho hoti … appamattakampi lohitaṁ duggandhaṁ hoti;
root_text.an1.329:1.2: evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, appamattakampi bhavaṁ na vaṇṇemi, antamaso accharāsaṅghātamattampi”.

translation_text.an1.316:1.1: “One individual, mendicants, arises in the world for the detriment and suffering of the people, against the people, for the harm, detriment, and suffering of gods and humans.
translation_text.an1.316:1.3: Someone with wrong view, whose perspective is distorted.
translation_text.an1.316:1.4: They draw many people away from the true teaching and establish them in false teachings.
translation_text.an1.316:1.5: This is one individual who arises in the world for the detriment and suffering of the people, against the people, for the harm, detriment, and suffering of gods and humans.”
translation_text.an1.317:1.1: “One individual, mendicants, arises in the world for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.an1.317:1.3: Someone with right view, whose perspective is undistorted.
translation_text.an1.317:1.4: They draw many people away from false teachings and establish them in the true teaching.
translation_text.an1.317:1.5: This is one individuals who arises in the world for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.”
translation_text.an1.318:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that is so very blameworthy as wrong view.
translation_text.an1.318:1.2: Wrong view is the most blameworthy thing of all.”
translation_text.an1.319:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single other individual who acts for the detriment and suffering of the people, against the people, for the harm, detriment, and suffering of gods and humans, as that futile man, the bamboo-staffed ascetic.
translation_text.an1.319:1.2: Just as a trap set at the mouth of a river would bring harm, suffering, calamity, and disaster for many fish,
translation_text.an1.319:1.3: so too that futile man, the bamboo-staffed ascetic, is a trap for humans, it seems to me. He has arisen in the world for the harm, suffering, calamity, and disaster of many beings.”
translation_text.an1.320:1.1: “Mendicants, the one who encourages someone in a poorly explained teaching and training, the one who they encourage, and the one who practices accordingly all brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an1.320:1.3: Because the teaching is poorly explained.”
translation_text.an1.321:1.1: “Mendicants, the one who encourages someone in a well explained teaching and training, the one who they encourage, and the one who practices accordingly all brim with much merit.
translation_text.an1.321:1.3: Because the teaching is well explained.”
translation_text.an1.322:1.1: “Mendicants, in a poorly explained teaching and training, the donor should know moderation, not the recipient.
translation_text.an1.322:1.3: Because the teaching is poorly explained.”
translation_text.an1.323:1.1: “Mendicants, in a well explained teaching and training, the recipient should know moderation, not the donor.
translation_text.an1.323:1.3: Because the teaching is well explained.”
translation_text.an1.324:1.1: “Mendicants, in a poorly explained teaching and training an energetic person lives in suffering.
translation_text.an1.324:1.3: Because the teaching is poorly explained.”
translation_text.an1.325:1.1: “Mendicants, in a well explained teaching and training a lazy person lives in suffering.
translation_text.an1.325:1.3: Because the teaching is well explained.”
translation_text.an1.326:1.1: “Mendicants, in a poorly explained teaching and training a lazy person lives happily.
translation_text.an1.326:1.3: Because the teaching is poorly explained.”
translation_text.an1.327:1.1: “Mendicants, in a well explained teaching and training an energetic person lives happily.
translation_text.an1.327:1.3: Because the teaching is well explained.”
translation_text.an1.328:1.1: “Just as, mendicants, even a little bit of fecal matter still stinks,
translation_text.an1.328:1.2: so too I don’t approve of even a little bit of continued existence, not even as long as a finger-snap.”
translation_text.an1.329:1.1: “Just as even a little bit of urine, or spit, or pus, or blood still stinks,
translation_text.an1.329:1.2: so too I don’t approve of even a little bit of continued existence, not even as long as a finger-snap.”

variant_text.an1.319:1.2: khippaṁ → khipaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | uḍḍeyya → oḍḍeyya (bj); ujjheyya (mr)
